task_objective.description: Design a social simulator to generate daily mobility trajectories of urban residents. A trajectory is an ordered sequence of (location, time) visits: {(l0,t0),(l1,t1),...,(ln,tn)}.
task_objective.simulation_focus:
Goal: generate one-day trajectories per resident (using LLM is optional).
Key latent factors: Pattern (habitual routines), Persona (semantic role), Motivation (day-specific intent).
Pipeline:
(1) From historical trajectories, derive Pattern (NL summary of routines/statistics) via LLM.
(2) From historical behavior, infer Persona (e.g., office worker/student/night-shift) via LLM.
(3) For a target date, summarize Motivation from the past 7 days via LLM.
(4) Prompt LLM with {Pattern, Persona, Motivation} to output a trajectory string in the SAME format as 1921Y.json (no rationale).
task_objective.Data Calibration Procedure:
Calibration loop (prompt-level optimization):
(1) Sample residents and target dates (2021 only).
(2) Predict target-day trajectory with the simulator.
(3) Compare with ground-truth and compute metrics (e.g., JSD over SD/SI, plus optional semantic distributions).
(4) Refine prompts used for Pattern extraction, Persona inference, and Motivation summarization.
NOTE: All calibration, validation, and evaluation are performed within 2021 with strict per-resident temporal separation (no future leakage).
task_objective.Simulator Design Requirements:
Build a simulator that generates daily trajectories.

CRITICAL SCOPE CONSTRAINT (STRICT):
- ONLY use 1921Y.json records whose dates fall in 2021-01-01 to 2021-12-31 (inclusive).
- ONLY predict target days whose dates fall in 2021-01-01 to 2021-12-31 (inclusive).
- Do NOT use any 2019 or 2020 records for training, calibration, validation, prompt building, model fitting, hyper-parameter selection, or evaluation.
- This task is strictly an in-regime 2021 setting: 2021 -> 2021.

Must support:
1) Data loading: load residents' daily trajectories from 1921Y.json and filter to 2021 only.
2) Pattern & Persona init: for each resident, generate and store Pattern + Persona using ONLY 2021 data, and ONLY from days prior to the prediction targets (no future leakage).
3) Train/validation split (within 2021): perform a per-resident temporal split of the resident's 2021 days into train/calibration vs validation targets; ensure validation targets have at least 7 prior days available (also within 2021).
4) Prediction: for each validation day (2021), compute Motivation from the past 7 days (2021 only and strictly before the target day); prompt LLM with {Pattern, Persona, Motivation}; output the predicted trajectory in the SAME STRING FORMAT as 1921Y.json (no rationale); evaluate against 2021 ground truth.
5) Strict separation: training/calibration and validation must be temporally separated per resident; never leak future days into Pattern/Persona/Motivation.

LLM output hard constraint (STRICT):
- Output MUST be a single string that exactly follows the 1921Y.json day-record format:
  "Activities at YYYY-MM-DD: POI#id at HH:MM:SS, POI#id at HH:MM:SS, ... ."
- Stops are comma-separated; preserve "POI#id" and HH:MM:SS; end with a period.
- Do NOT include rationale/reasons, extra fields, markdown, or JSON.
task_objective.Experimental settings:
ONLY ONE setting is allowed:
- 2021 -> 2021 (in-regime 2021)

DISALLOWED:
- 2019/2020 -> 2019/2020
- 2019/2020 -> 2021 (OOD)
- 2021 -> 2019/2020

If 2019 or 2020 records exist in 1921Y.json, they must be ignored completely for this task.
task_objective.Input-Output Mapping of the Simulated Ag:
Input: resident history up to the 7 days before target date, restricted to 2021 only; optional contextual cue for the target date (can be blank).
Output: a single trajectory string in the same format as 1921Y.json:
"Activities at YYYY-MM-DD: POI#id at HH:MM:SS, POI#id at HH:MM:SS, ... ." (no rationale/reasons; no JSON).
Evaluation: compare generated vs real trajectories for 2021 only; compute JSD between SD and SI distributions (and optionally DARD/STVD).

data_files.1921Y.json:
Resident daily trajectories across multiple years. For THIS TASK, only use dates in 2021.
Format:
- Key: resident_id (e.g., "1874").
- Value: list of day strings, each begins with "Activities at YYYY-MM-DD:" followed by comma-separated stops.
- Stop unit: "POI#id at HH:MM:SS" (arrival time at that POI).

STRICT FILTERING RULE:
- When loading, parse the date after "Activities at" and keep ONLY records where date is within [2021-01-01, 2021-12-31].
- All training/calibration/validation/evaluation must be performed ONLY on this filtered subset.
- Any 2019/2020 (or other-year) records must be ignored completely.

Linking:
- Category lookup: strip suffix "#id" from "POI#id" -> "POI"; map "POI" to category via catto.json.
- Coordinates lookup: use full "POI#id" to retrieve (lat, lon) from poi_category_192021_longitude_latitude_complement_alignment_clean.json.

Output format is strict; follow the exact tokens and separators shown above.
data_files.poi_category_192021_longitude_latitude.j: Maps each full POI name (e.g., "Rest Area#1061") to its latitude/longitude. Use it to get coordinates for each visited stop (including those in 2021).
data_files.catto.json: Maps base POI name (suffix removed; e.g., "Rest Area") to a semantic category (e.g., "Travel & Transport"). Use it to compute category-based metrics (e.g., DARD).

category_share_mae.description: Mean absolute error between the generated and ground-truth distributions of visit category shares (computed from stop categories mapped via catto.json) on the evaluated target set. Lower is better.

stop_count_abs_mean_error.description: Absolute mean error of the number of stops per day between generated and ground-truth trajectories on the evaluated target set. Lower is better.

stop_count_kl.description: Kullback-Leibler divergence between the generated and ground-truth distributions of daily stop counts on the evaluated target set. Lower is better.

tod_jsd_avg.description: Average Jensen-Shannon divergence between generated and ground-truth time-of-day (ToD) distributions of stop arrival times on the evaluated target set. Lower is better.

topk_poi_recall.description: Recall@K of top-K most frequently visited POIs: fraction of ground-truth top-K POIs that appear in generated trajectories (aggregated over the evaluated target set). Higher is better.

transition_divergence.description: Divergence between generated and ground-truth transition behavior (e.g., POI-to-POI or category-to-category transition distributions) on the evaluated target set. Lower is better.

trip_distance_wasserstein.description: Wasserstein distance between generated and ground-truth trip distance distributions (computed from consecutive-stop geographic distances using poi_category_192021_longitude_latitude.json) on the evaluated target set. Lower is better.
